rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. The image clearly shows 'Summa Receptoram et Solutorum.' as a distinct top title, separate from the date. Visually verified 'Receptoram' spelling. gpt-5-mini incorrectly merged this line with the date below it.

Selected gemini-flash. This is a distinct header line from the image, correctly separated from the main title. gpt-5-mini had merged this with the first row's description, causing a row count discrepancy and an 'added missing' row in the supervisor's output.
Consensus across both extractors. Clearly a section header, with no associated amounts.
Consensus across both extractors for description and amounts. Values are clearly legible in the image.
rows.english_description: Receipts of arrears (outstanding payments previously due).

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash for the description 'Anni praesentis', which is visually verified in the image (gpt-5-mini had 'Anni praesent'). Amounts are consistent between extractors and visually confirmed, including the '½' fraction.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipts for the present year, amounting to £209 15s 4½d.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash for the description 'Varia --', which accurately includes the two trailing dashes visible in the image (gpt-5-mini omitted them). Amounts are consistent between extractors and visually confirmed, including the '½' fraction.
Consensus across both extractors. Clearly an underlined subtotal with legible amounts.
Consensus across both extractors for description and amounts. Values are clearly legible in the image.
rows.english_description: Payments made to the Rector, to the scholars, and similar recipients.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payments made to the Rector and to scholars, amounting to £520 14s 3d.

rows.notes: Consensus across both extractors for description and amounts. Values are clearly legible in the image.
Selected gemini-flash. Description 'Varia --' is visually verified (gpt-5-mini omitted dashes). The amount for pounds is clearly '1568' in the image; gpt-5-mini misread it as '1588'.
Consensus across both extractors. Clearly an underlined subtotal with legible amounts.

Selected gemini-flash for the description 'Debita Anni praecedentis', which is visually verified in the image (gpt-5-mini had 'precedentis'). Amounts are consistent between extractors and visually confirmed.
rows.english_description: Payments of debts from the preceding year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payments of debts of the preceding year, amounting to £126 18s 2d.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Both extractors identified the correct description and pounds/shillings. The pence column is blank; gemini-flash interprets this as '0', which is standard practice for ledger totals, while gpt-5-mini left it blank with noted uncertainty. This line is double-underlined in the image.
Consensus across both extractors. This row carries forward a previous total and is clearly legible.
rows.english_description: Amount taken from the receipts (transfer from receipts).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Amount taken from the receipts, amounting to £3003 9s 4d.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Similar to row 13, both extractors correctly identified description, pounds, and shillings. The pence column is blank; gemini-flash interprets this as '0', which is standard ledger practice, while gpt-5-mini left it blank with noted uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Amount deducted for payments (transfer representing payments made).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Amount deducted for payments, amounting to £2854 2s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus across both extractors. Clearly a double-underlined final balance with legible amounts.
Consensus across both extractors. Clearly a header for the approval/signature section.
Selected gemini-flash. Visually verified 'I.' (not 'J.') and 'Rectorem' (not 'Rectorum'). The row type 'entry' is more appropriate for a specific signatory than 'header' (gpt-5-mini).
rows.english_description: Signature of I. L. Richards, the Rector, confirming the accounts.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Signature of I. L. Richards, the Rector. No monetary amount recorded.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Visually verified 'Joannem' (not 'Joannen') and the trailing period. This line is clearly legible, contrary to gpt-5-mini's lower confidence and notes on faintness. Row type 'entry' is more appropriate.
rows.english_description: Signature of John Ley, the Sub-rector, confirming the accounts.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Signature of John Ley, the Sub-rector. No monetary amount recorded.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Visually verified 'Gulielmum Sevele Decanum.'. gpt-5-mini had a significant misread ('Julianum / Sevels') and inappropriately low confidence. Row type 'entry' is more appropriate.
rows.english_description: Signature of William Sevele, the Dean, confirming the accounts.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Signature of William Sevele, the Dean. No monetary amount recorded.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Visually confirmed 'Gulielmum W. Wathecombe Commensale.'. The surname 'Wathecombe' has minor ambiguity (reflected in confidence) but is legible. gpt-5-mini had a major misread ('Woolhouse') and inappropriately low confidence. Row type 'entry' is more appropriate.
rows.english_description: Signature of William W. Wathecombe, a commensal (college member), confirming the accounts.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Signature of William W. Wathecombe, a commensal. No monetary amount recorded.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Visually confirmed 'Gulielmum Andrews Socius'. gpt-5-mini had significant misreads ('Anders Saum') and inappropriately low confidence. This line is clearly legible. Row type 'entry' is more appropriate.
rows.english_description: Signature of William Andrews, a fellow, confirming the accounts.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Signature of William Andrews, a fellow. No monetary amount recorded.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash. Visually confirmed 'Robertum S. Sattor Bursarium Quaestorem'. gpt-5-mini had significant misreads ('Sutton', 'Brasarium') and inappropriately low confidence, incorrectly noting faintness. This line is clearly legible. Row type 'entry' is more appropriate.
